title: Atos AC²E Framework
lead: The Atos Circle of Customer Excellence (AC²E) is a recognised standard framework for shaping client engagements — centred on business excellence and structured around six interconnected business aspects within their wider context.

sections.heading: The AC²E Model
sections.alt: Atos AC²E model — Business Excellence at the centre, surrounded by six business aspects and an outer ring of context factors

sections.heading: What is AC²E?
sections.body: Atos Amplify engagements are focused on improving the business excellence of the customer — understanding the aspects that contribute to excellence and considering the customer's broader business context. The AC²E framework provides a recognised standard to shape these engagements consistently and comprehensively.

items.desc: Strategic objectives — the goals and ambitions that define what excellence means for this organisation
Market and customer demand — the external pull that shapes priorities and investment
KPIs — the measurable outcomes that define whether excellence is being achieved
operating model, governance, and organisational identity that enable delivery
capabilities, behaviours, and cultural mindset required for transformation
end-to-end processes and value streams that deliver customer outcomes
data strategy, governance, and quality that underpin decision-making and AI
application landscape, modernisation, and integration supporting business processes
infrastructure, cloud, and platform capabilities that enable scalable delivery
Market developments — trends and competitive dynamics that influence the engagement scope
Regulations — compliance requirements and legal constraints that bound the solution space
Security & Safety — risk, cyber, and safety requirements embedded across all aspects
Environmental awareness — sustainability goals and ESG constraints shaping decisions

sections.heading: Positioning: When to Apply AC²E
sections.alt: AC²E Goal — positioning within the 6D delivery model (Define, Discover, Design, Develop, Deliver, Drive)

items.desc: shape a project in terms of goals (customer KPIs) and scope (internal and external)
check completeness of the project vision across all six business aspects
select techniques and tools appropriate to the aspects in scope
check solution quality-of-service against the defined business aspects
check outcomes against original project goals and business excellence targets
reassess continuously for possible new developments in the outer context ring
